title: Combat Application Tourniquet (CAT)
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- Uncontrolled, life threatening haemorrhage of a limb.

#### Procedure

- Apply the tourniquet 5-10 cm proximal to the bleeding site. Route the band around the limb and pass the tip through the buckle. Pull the band **very tight** and firmly fasten the band back on itself (see **Figure 1**).

- Grasp the windlass rod and twist until the haemorrhage has been controlled. 
- Once the desired tightness has been achieved, place the rod inside the plastic clip (see **Figure 2**)
- Reassess for bleeding and distal pulse
- If required, consider additional tightening of the tourniquet or the application of a second tourniquet proximal to the first.

- Secure the rod inside the clip with the ‘Time’ strap and record the time of application on the same strap. 
- **Constantly** reassess for bleeding and distal pulse.

Figure 1

Figure 2

#### Specific Notes

- This is a procedure of last resort - saving a **life** or saving a **limb**.
- If a CAT is not available, utilise a manual BP cuff or Triangular bandage to construct a makeshift tourniquet. All efforts must be exhausted to arrest the life threatening haemorrhage.
- CAT 6 tourniquets with dual friction belt should no longer be stocked. If found, ensure they are removed and replaced with generation 7 tourniquet (with single loop buckle).
- **Once the tourniquet is applied and haemorrhage is controlled, the tourniquet should not be released or removed.**
